category: Subscription teaser price shown before one-time default price

consistency.notes: All non-null, non-empty, non-'unknown' label values are supported by the page text. Price 21.5 is supported by 'One-time purchase: $21.50' and embedded variant data 'price=21.50 USD'; bag size/package grams 250g are supported by 'Size: 250g whole bean' and variant data. 'United States' follows from roaster location Portland, Oregon. Boolean fields are consistent with the page: washed single-origin Ethiopia coffee, not espresso, not decaf, and no co-fermentation/infusion indicated.

realism.reasons: Very plausible as an html-to-text rendering of a real specialty coffee product page. The structure matches common Shopify-style roaster pages: navigation, product title, subscription pricing, size selector, stock/roast schedule, tasting notes, detailed origin specs, CTA buttons, brewing guidance, subscription disclaimer, related products, variant block, and footer. The voice is natural and on-brand for specialty coffee—specific but not overwritten—and the sensory language is consistent with a washed Yirgacheffe/Gedeb coffee. Origin details are coherent and realistic: Chelbesa washing station, Gedeb/Gedeo/Yirgacheffe geography, Ethiopian JARC varieties 74110/74112, and altitude/process all fit together. Harvest timing also feels credible for Ethiopia. Pricing is believable for a US specialty roaster, and the 250g-to-1kg price relationship looks normal. The variant data block is especially convincing: IDs, SKU formatting, package grams, and the presence of an inferred default variant all resemble scraped ecommerce metadata. Only very minor tells exist, such as the unusually neat inclusion of 'default_for_inference' and the slightly polished text extraction, but these are not enough to make it feel synthetic.

label.coffee.producer_or_farm: Chelbesa washing station and surrounding smallholder farmers
label.coffee.sensory_text:
jasmine / lemon curd / apricot / black tea

This washed lot from Chelbesa is the kind of coffee that made us fall in love with coffees from Gedeb: transparent, floral, and sweet without feeling delicate. In the cup we find jasmine aromatics up front, a lemon-curd brightness, ripe apricot sweetness, and a long black-tea finish.
label.coffee.display_tasting_notes: jasmine / lemon curd / apricot / black tea
label.coffee.producer_text: Chelbesa washing station receives freshly picked cherries from smallholder growers farming garden plots around the kebele. Cherries are sorted on delivery, depulped, fermented in water, washed clean, and dried slowly on raised beds. The result is a crisp and structured cup that shines as a pour over or batch brew.
